question_text: A balloon is rubbed with a wool cloth and becomes negatively charged with 8 million excess electrons. How many coulombs of charge does the balloon have? (The charge of one electron is 1.6 × 10^-19 coulombs.)
explanation: To find the total charge, multiply the number of electrons by the charge per electron: 8 × 10^6 × 1.6 × 10^-19 = 12.8 × 10^-13 = 1.28 × 10^-12 coulombs. This shows how even millions of electrons only produce a tiny amount of charge.
